evidence.evidence_text: rushing, the shells are ﬂaky and irregularly shaped. For preparing CS aggregates, a crusher was designed, manufactured, and in- stalled at SRM University. Since CS is a ﬂaky material, the sizes of CS lengthwise are restricted to a maximum of 12 mm ( Fig. 1), to get enough workability with the con- crete. The surface texture of the shell was fairly smooth on concave and rough on convex faces. The crushed edges were rough and spiky. CS aggregates have a rela- tively high water absorption value nearly, 24%, compared to the conventional aggregate (0.5%); hence, to prevent water absorption by the concrete mix, it is nec- essary to mix at Saturated Surface Dry condition (SSD) based on 24 h submersion in potable water. The CS was examined using SEM and analyzed. The SEM images on CS are shown in Fig. 2. Fig. 3 shows SEM images on CS without and with soaking of 24 h duration. 4.2. Other concrete mix constituents Ordinary Portland Cement (OPC) 53 Grade conforming to Indian Standard IS 12269: 1987 [8] is used as a binder. River sand (Palar river bed)
